response.justification: Sarah exceeds the experience requirement with 10+ years of full-stack development and a current Staff-level role at re:cinq. She demonstrates strong alignment with the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, proven testing culture, MongoDB) and has specific expertise in AI agent development and MCP frameworks—a required skill that's often difficult to find. Main gaps are lack of explicit mention of React, Vitest, and Nest.js, though her deep full-stack background and current AI infrastructure work suggest she can quickly acquire these specific technologies.
strengths: Current Staff Software Engineer at re:cinq with active experience in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators—directly matches the AI agent development requirement
10+ years of full-stack development across TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, and MongoDB with proven ability to pick up new languages and frameworks rapidly

Strong engineering culture demonstrated through TDD advocacy, open-source contributions (GISCollective), teaching (workshops and courses), and roles at architecture-focused companies like RIDE Capital and Optilyz
concerns: React, Vitest, and Nest.js not explicitly mentioned on resume despite being primary stack technologies—unclear if candidate has hands-on production experience with these specific tools
Current role at re:cinq appears heavily focused on AI/infrastructure and tooling rather than traditional full-stack feature development, raising questions about recent frontend/React depth
PostgreSQL and GCP/Azure not mentioned; database and cloud platform experience appears limited to MongoDB and AWS, leaving gaps in the secondary stack requirements

key_factors.factor: Primary stack technology gaps (React, Vitest, Nest.js)
